name: testing-fundamentals
description: Auto-invoke when reviewing test files or discussing testing strategy. Enforces testing pyramid, strategic coverage, and stack-appropriate frameworks.
capabilities: Enforce testing pyramid (70% unit, 20% integration, 10% E2E)
Recommend stack-appropriate frameworks (Vitest, Jest, pytest, go test)
Identify testing anti-patterns (implementation testing, over-mocking, brittle selectors)
Guide test structure using AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert)
Provide Socratic questions for testing strategy discussions
Flag testing red flags during code review
author: DanielPodolsky
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown guidance on testing practices. No executable code exists - no JavaScript, Python, Ruby, or shell scripts were found. All 37 static findings are false positives: hash values and metadata were misidentified as cryptographic weaknesses; markdown code block markers (```) were flagged as shell backticks; ASCII art arrows (▲ ╱ ╲) in the testing pyramid diagram triggered false alarms. This skill provides testing strategy guidance only and poses no security risk.
valueStatement: Teams often write tests that do not catch bugs or become maintenance burdens. This skill provides the testing pyramid, framework guidance, and anti-patterns to write tests that prove your code works.
limitations: Does not execute or generate test code
Does not run existing tests or report coverage
Does not integrate with CI/CD pipelines

useCases.title: Review test quality
useCases.description: Apply this skill when reviewing pull requests to ensure tests follow the pyramid and avoid anti-patterns.

useCases.title: Define testing strategy
useCases.description: Use the framework guide to select appropriate testing tools for your stack and establish team standards.

useCases.title: Learn testing concepts
useCases.description: Learn what to test, how to structure tests, and which questions to ask when writing tests.

promptTemplates.title: Review test quality
promptTemplates.prompt: Review these test files for quality. Check if they follow the testing pyramid, avoid anti-patterns, and test behavior not implementation.

promptTemplates.title: Framework guidance
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the best testing framework for a Vite + React project? What about for Node.js native ESM?

promptTemplates.title: Test strategy discussion
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me plan tests for this feature. What should be unit tests, what should be integration, and what critical flows need E2E?

promptTemplates.title: Fix test patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: This test is brittle and testing implementation details. Help me rewrite it to test user-facing behavior.

bestPractices: Follow the testing pyramid: 70% unit, 20% integration, 10% E2E
Test user behavior, not implementation details (use getByRole, not getByClassName)
Mock only external boundaries, not your own code
antiPatterns: Testing internal state instead of what the user sees
Mocking everything and losing confidence in tests
Using getByClassName selectors that break with CSS changes

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill recommend?
faq.answer: Vitest for Vite projects, Jest for CRA, pytest for Python, go test for Go, and Playwright for E2E across stacks.

faq.question: What coverage percentage should I target?
faq.answer: Strategic coverage focused on critical paths. 100% coverage often creates tests that exist just for metrics.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests for me?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance on testing strategy and patterns. It does not execute or generate code.

faq.question: Is my test data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only reads text for guidance. No test data is accessed, transmitted, or stored.

faq.question: Why is my test breaking after refactoring?
faq.answer: You may be testing implementation details. Rewrite to test user-facing behavior that stays stable across refactors.

faq.question: How is this different from AI code generators?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on testing strategy and review, not code generation. It asks questions to guide better decisions.
